title: brassica gravinae
intro: Brassica gravinae, also known as wild cabbage or sea kale, is a type of plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is native to coastal areas of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Brassica gravinae include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. Herbal preparations of Brassica gravinae include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
